terms.definition: A simulated technical or behavioural interview run in realistic conditions to rehearse the *behaviour* of interviewing.
A mock focused on a single algorithmic problem solved under a clock with live narration.
A mock focused on a single open-ended architecture question, exercising tradeoff reasoning and scope management.
A mock of past-project / leadership / collaboration questions using STAR (Situation-Task-Action-Result) responses.
Behavioural-answer template that structures stories as Situation, Task, Action, Result.
The opening minutes of a coding interview spent narrowing the problem with targeted questions before coding.
Post-mock conversation where partners exchange structured feedback; the highest-signal phase of the whole exercise.
A back-to-back sequence of mocks (coding + design + behavioural) approximating the stress of an actual hiring loop.
